title: Parsons Console Bookshelf
summary: A three-shelf console built Parsons-style — solid end panels flush to the shelves rather than legs set back from the edges — trimmed in 2x2 for a clean, boxy look.
description:
A true Parsons piece has its end panels flush with the front and top edges, no legs set back or shaped — this console is that same flush, boxy silhouette built from 1x12 shelves and 2x2 end panels rather than a solid slab, which keeps the weight and material cost down while keeping the look. Pocket-hole the shelf sides and attach 2x2 trim to the shelf fronts first, keeping the trim flush with the shelf tops.

Build both end panels identically from 2x2 stock joined with pocket holes and glue, matching the height to whatever look you're after. **Assembly is genuinely just two steps after that**: screw and glue the ends to the shelves, and you're done — there's no face frame, no door, no drawer to fuss over.

**This design scales cleanly into a tall tower**, simply by lengthening the legs and shelves to a taller, narrower proportion — the same flush-panel technique carries straight through at any height, since nothing about the joinery changes with the size.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Trim the shelves
steps.body: Drill pocket holes in the sides of each shelf. Attach the shelf trim to the shelf fronts and ends, keeping the trim flush with the shelf tops.

steps.title: Build the end panels
steps.body: Build two identical end panels from the leg pieces and shelf end trim, joined with pocket hole screws and glue.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.body: Attach the end panels to the shelves with pocket hole screws and glue, keeping everything flush and square.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: Parsons-style console bookshelf with flush end panels
